intent: Restore spec composted earlier today. Phase 0: Form-native emitter writing real Python via the kernel's write_file_text host call. First emitted module: kernels/python_bmf/objects.py.

what_changed.form_native_emitter: form/form-stdlib/emits/python-native.fk — produces Python source via write_file_text
what_changed.emitter_script: form/scripts/emit_native_python.sh — source-compiles core.fk then runs the kernel against the emitter
what_changed.diff_tool: form/scripts/diff_form_artifacts.py — structural diff with artifact-local id remapping
what_changed.sdk_bridge: kernels/python_bmf/sdk.py — hand-tuned (NodeID, intern, SourceSpan, .fkb read/write, Lens)
what_changed.emitted_module: kernels/python_bmf/objects.py — produced by the Form emitter via write_file_text

false_path_caught: First pass hand-wrote objects.py / parser.py / rules.py / section_parser.py / form_action.py / compiler.py in kernels/python_bmf/ as 'destination shape'. User caught it: same false-start pattern as the previously-composted attempt — different surface, same sin. Hand-written compiler modules deleted. Only sdk.py / __init__.py / README.md remain hand-tuned; every other .py is emitter output.

proof.command: form/scripts/emit_native_python.sh && python3 -m py_compile kernels/python_bmf/*.py && python3 -c 'from kernels.python_bmf.objects import PyBmfCategory, py_keyword; assert int(PyBmfCategory.IMPORT) == 501; assert int(PyBmfCategory.MATCH_AS) == 574; assert py_keyword("def").value == "def"'
proof.result: PASS — Form recipes ran through form-kernel-go, wrote kernels/python_bmf/objects.py via write_file_text, the Python compiles, imports, and the IntEnum/atoms work correctly. PyBmfCategory.IMPORT == 501, PyBmfCategory.MATCH_AS == 574, 74 categories total.

next_breaths: Phase 1: extend pn-categories to read from python-bmf.fk's compiled .fkb via read_form_binary + lens lookup so divergence is impossible.
Phase 2: pn-emit-parser-module — port python-source-scan-text + layout derivation.
Phase 3: pn-emit-rules-module — walk python-bmf-rules, render each as Rule dataclass entry.
Phase 5: parity suite + perf comparison; populate scripts/perf_compare_native_python.sh findings.
